item.title: Netanyahu made secret visit to UAE to meet with country's president
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made a secret visit to the United Arab Emirates recently, where he met with Mohammed bin Zayed, the country's president.

item.title: Iran insists it's ready for a new U.S. attack as Trump arrives in China
item.description: Iran's military says it's trained and ready for any new U.S. assault as Trump predicts a "long talk" about the war with China's President Xi in Beijing.

item.title: Netanyahu confirms secret visit to UAE
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made a secret visit to the United Arab Emirates recently, his office confirmed Wednesday. CBS News Middle East contributor Courtney Kealy has more.

item.title: Amid fraud probe, Medicare pauses new enrollments for hospice providers
item.description: The Trump administration announced Wednesday that it would be put a six-month moratorium on new Medicare enrollments by hospice and home health agencies to target fraud.

item.title: How to post jobs online and reach more qualified candidates
item.description: Finding the right job candidates can be a tough task, but there are simple ways to improve the hiring process.

item.title: Guantanamo detention center nearly empty after Trump said U.S. would use it house 30,000 migrants
item.description: President Trump said at the beginning of his second presidential term that the U.S. would hold 30,000 migrants at the Guantanamo Bay detention center in Cuba, but the facility remains mostly empty. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: What does the new inflation surge mean for mortgage interest rates?
item.description: Inflation is now at its highest level in three years. Here's what that could mean for mortgage interest rates.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders a new trial
item.description: The South Carolina Supreme Court overturned the murder convictions against Alex Murdaugh and ordered a new trial. Murdaugh was found guilty in 2023 of killing his wife and son in 2021. The court questioned "improper" conduct by the county clerk, Becky Hill, who later wrote a book about her time working on the trial. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: IDPH investigating possible hantavirus in Winnebago Co. not linked to cruise
item.description: The Illinois Department of Public Health said it is investigating a potential case of hantavirus in an Illinois resident, that they said is not linked to the deadly cruise ship outbreak.

item.title: Bear kills worker at uranium mining facility in Canada
item.description: A black bear killed a man working for a Canadian uranium exploration and acquisition company in Saskatchewan.

item.title: Senate defeats 7th attempt to limit Trump on Iran, despite new GOP defection
item.description: The Senate rejected another attempt by Democrats to limit President Trump's ability to use military force against Iran, but one new Republican senator voted in favor of advancing the measure.

item.title: Justice Department investigating oil market trading, sources say
item.description: The Justice Department is probing suspicious trading timed to market swings, two sources say.

item.title: Top U.S. CEOs worth nearly $1 trillion accompany Trump to China
item.description: The delegation of business leaders underscores the deep ties many major U.S. companies maintain with China despite years of trade tensions.

item.title: How much of my Social Security can be garnished for debt?
item.description: If you're a retiree with unpaid debt, it's important to know how a garnishment could impact your monthly benefits.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, orders new trial
item.description: Alex Murdaugh was convicted of his killing his wife and his son at the family's home in 2021.

item.title: Overdose deaths fall for 3rd straight year, CDC data shows
item.description: This marks the longest decline in overdose deaths in decades, according to preliminary government data.

item.title: $100,000 short-term CD vs. $100,000 long-term CD: Which will earn more interest now?
item.description: With interest rates likely to stay higher for longer, it helps to know the interest-earning potential of a $100,000 CD now.

item.title: Social Security's cost-of-living adjustment could reach nearly 4% in 2027
item.description: A larger COLA would boost monthly checks for retirees, but also strain Social Security's already depleted trust funds.

item.title: Skin cancer cases rising in the U.S., report says
item.description: New data on skin cancer in the U.S. makes a stronger case for monitoring and preventative measures. Joseph Sardano, the CEO of Sensus Healthcare, joins CBS News with more details.

item.title: How the Iran war could impact Trump's high-stakes meeting with China's Xi
item.description: President Trump is meeting in Beijing with Chinese President Xi Jinping in a highly-anticipated summit. The two world leaders are expected to discuss Iran, Taiwan, trade and more. Weijia Jiang has the latest.

item.title: Trump arrives in China as Iran war looms over summit with Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump touched down in Beijing on Wednesday ahead of his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Courtney Kealy and Ramy Inocencio have the latest.

item.title: Geena Davis on "The Boroughs" and why she's drawn to supernatural projects
item.description: Actor Geena Davis talks about starring in the new series "The Boroughs," if there are parallels between herself and the character she plays, and why she's drawn to supernatural projects. She also addresses representation in the entertainment industry.

item.title: Menacing "monster wolf" robots in high demand to scare off bears
item.description: "Monster Wolf" is an animatronic scarecrow with flashing red eyes that howls and growls menacingly to scare away wild animals.

item.title: How diet and nutrition may help boost your brain health
item.description: Alzheimer's Association CEO and president Joanne Pike shares some recommendations on which foods to eat, and which foods to limit, for better brain health. (Sponsored by the Alzheimer's Association.)

item.title: Shots fired in Philippines Senate, where a wanted senator is holed up
item.description: Sen. Ronald dela Rosa of the Philippines is wanted by the International Criminal Court for his alleged role in the killings of at least 32 people.

item.title: Trump promised to hold 30,000 migrants at Guantanamo. It's mostly empty.
item.description: A CBS News review of internal government documents and information provided to Congress shows immigration detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay are nearly empty.

item.title: Judge shot dead while heading to gym without her bodyguards
item.description: Lady Pachar was shot that day while traveling by car to a gym in the southwestern city of Machala.

item.title: Businesses starting to receive their IEEPA tariff refunds
item.description: "I'd been checking the status feverishly to see if anything was in my bank account," one small business owner said.

item.title: Canvas' parent company strikes deal with hackers to delete stolen data
item.description: A hacking group named ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for the Canvas breach and threatened to leak data involving 275 million individuals if schools did not pay a ransom.

item.title: How Uber, DoorDash and Lyft accounts are being rented and sold online
item.description: CBS News California Investigates found that accounts for companies such as Uber, Lyft and DoorDash can be bought or rented online without needing to provide identification.
